title: Lazy-Sunday Mexican Cabbage Churros
description: Floral, molten, lazy Sunday Mexican dish featuring savoy cabbage, fry-grind-rice-khao-khua, jain-friendly, form=churros.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, sugar, cornstarch, baking powder, baking soda, and a pinch of salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the vegan milk, coconut oil, vanilla extract, and a pinch of cinnamon.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Cover the dough with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Preheat the oil in a deep frying pan or a deep fryer to 375°F (190°C).
Once the oil is hot, use a piping bag or a plastic bag with a corner cut off to pipe the dough into the hot oil.
Fry the churros for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and puffed up.
Remove the churros from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
In a small bowl, mix together the coconut milk, brown sugar, and a pinch of cinnamon.
Dust the churros with the mixture and serve warm.
Optional: Melt the vegan chocolate and drizzle it over the churros before serving.
Optional: Sprinkle the churros with chopped fresh cilantro, lime juice, and a pinch of salt for added flavor.
Optional: Serve the churros with a side of fried rice grains, khao khua, and a drizzle of sesame oil for added texture and flavor.
Optional: Garnish the churros with chopped fresh mint, basil, or a sprinkle of saffron threads for added flavor and color.
Optional: Serve the churros with a side of warm piloncillo sauce for dipping.
Optional: Top the churros with a sprinkle of ground cinnamon, anise seeds, or a pinch of ground cumin for added flavor and spice.
Optional: Serve the churros with a side of warm ginger syrup for dipping.
Optional: Dust the churros with a pinch of ground cardamom, ground coriander, or a pinch of ground cayenne pepper for added flavor and spice.
Optional: Serve the churros with a side of warm star anise syrup for dipping.
Optional: Top the churros with a sprinkle of chopped fresh basil, chopped fresh cilantro, or a pinch of chopped fresh mint for added flavor and color.
